exampleSentences: Anh ấy cảm thấy xấu khi làm sai điều gì đó.
Nếu thời tiết xấu, chúng ta sẽ ở nhà hôm nay.
Cô giáo nói hành động nói dối là xấu và không nên làm.
notes:
xấu = bad. It describes something negative, wrong, low quality, or unpleasant.

It’s an adjective, so it goes after the noun: người xấu (bad person), tin xấu (bad news), kết quả xấu (bad result). It can describe behavior, situations, health, luck, or appearance.

It also commonly means ugly (about looks): xấu ↔ đẹp (beautiful). For “bad” in a moral sense, it contrasts with tốt (good).

Very flexible word — if something isn’t good, nice, or pretty, xấu often fits.

definitions.vi: Không dễ nhìn; không hấp dẫn.
definitions.es: No agradable a la vista; poco atractivo.
definitions.en: Not pleasant to look at; unattractive.

example.vi: Cô ấy có một nụ cười xấu.

exampleSentencesTranslated: He feels bad when he does something wrong.
If the weather is bad, we will stay at home today.
The teacher says lying is bad and should not be done.
notes_md:
**xấu** = bad. It describes something negative, wrong, low quality, or unpleasant.

It’s an **adjective**, so it goes after the noun: *người xấu* (bad person), *tin xấu* (bad news), *kết quả xấu* (bad result). It can describe behavior, situations, health, luck, or appearance.

It also commonly means **ugly** (about looks): *xấu* ↔ *đẹp* (beautiful). For “bad” in a moral sense, it contrasts with *tốt* (good).

Very flexible word — if something isn’t good, nice, or pretty, *xấu* often fits.
